title: Chinese Warship Shandong Conducts Port Call in Hong Kong
summary: Chinese aircraft carrier Shandong arrives in Hong Kong for five-day port visit. Public tours thousands of visitors aboard carrier. Event showcases PLA Navy capabilities amid regional tensions.
contents:
## Chinese Warship Shandong Conducts Port Call in Hong Kong

### Overview
Chinese aircraft carrier Shandong arrives in Hong Kong for five-day port visit. Public tours thousands of visitors aboard carrier. Event showcases PLA Navy capabilities amid regional tensions.

### Event Details
Chinese aircraft carrier Shandong docks in Hong Kong on April 24, 2026, for a five-day goodwill port call. Thousands of locals tour the vessel, exploring flight deck, hangars, and J-15 fighter jets. PLA Navy opens decks to public, highlighting advanced carrier operations and crew readiness. Event coincides with 25th anniversary of Hong Kong handover, drawing crowds eager to photograph warships up close. Shandong, China's first domestically built carrier, displaces 70,000 tons and carries 40+ aircraft. Tour guides explain catapult launches, arrestor wires, and electromagnetic systems. Visitors snap photos of helicopters, missiles, and radar arrays. Local media covers event extensively, interviewing excited attendees. Beijing leverages visit to project naval power in South China Sea disputes. Tour runs through April 28, with slots filling rapidly online. Security personnel monitor crowds while sailors interact with guests. Shandong departs Hong Kong afterward, resuming patrols. Event underscores China's expanding blue-water navy ambitions. Analysts note timing amid US carrier transits nearby. Public enthusiasm reflects national pride in military modernization. WarshipCam captures stunning images of carrier moored at Victoria Harbour, drawing global attention to PLA Navy strides.

### Source Context
@WarshipCam (WarshipCam) covers this category of developments. The account focuses on warship naval vessel photography russian chinese iranian deployment port call.
